data.content:
# Implementation Plan — foolery-7192

## Root cause

`buildSetlistPlanPreview` (`src/lib/setlist-chart.ts:94-112`) returns
`totalBeats: beatIds.length` — every beat id, regardless of state.
`PlanSummaryCard` (`src/components/setlist-view.tsx:414`) renders
`selectedWorkableBeatCount ?? preview.totalBeats`. When the row is
unselected, the fallback is `preview.totalBeats`, so the badge counts
shipped/abandoned/closed knots as if they were still pending. Selecting
the row swaps in `countWorkableSetlistRows(chart)`
(`src/lib/setlist-chart.ts:157-163`), which filters
`TERMINAL_SETLIST_STATES = {"shipped","abandoned","closed"}` — that's
why selecting "fixes" the number.

`useSetlistBaseData` already loads every repo beat (with state) into
`beatMap` (`src/components/setlist-view.tsx:282-291`), so the
information needed for an accurate unselected count is already on the
client. No server change is required for criteria 1–2.

The execution_plan workflow lives at
`/Users/cartine/knots/loom/execution_plan_sdlc/workflow.loom` —
positive terminal is `shipped`. Plan artifact state is exposed via
`PlanSummary.artifact.state`
(`src/lib/orchestration-plan-types.ts:34-41`). The "Complete" button
(criterion 3) needs a new server route that forces the plan knot to
`shipped` via `KnotsBackend.close(..., { force: true })` — per
CLAUDE.md, this is the only sanctioned correction primitive that may
skip workflow gates.

## Files I will touch

### Library
- `src/lib/setlist-chart.ts`
  - Add `countWorkableBeatIds(beatIds, beatMap): number` —
    canonical helper that filters via `isTerminalSetlistState`.
    Single source of truth used by both unselected and selected paths.
  - Add `TERMINAL_PLAN_ARTIFACT_STATES = new Set(["shipped","abandoned"])`
    plus `isTerminalPlanArtifactState(state)`. Drives Done badge and
    Complete-button visibility (criteria 2 + 3).
  - Add a `workableBeats: number` field to `SetlistPlanPreview` and
    populate it in `buildSetlistPlanPreview` using the new helper.
    Leave `totalBeats` for backward compatibility within this knot's
    diff; do not delete `previewBeats`/`remainingBeats` (out of scope
    — already-retired display branch guarded by source contract test).

### UI
- `src/components/setlist-view.tsx`
  - In `LoadedSetlistView`/`SetlistSummaryPanel`, replace the chart-
    based `selectedWorkableBeatCount` with the same
    `countWorkableBeatIds(plan.beatIds, beatMap)` helper applied per
    summary. Rename the prop accordingly (`workableBeatCountByPlan:
    Map<string, number>`) — both unselected and selected rows now
    read from the same map, guaranteeing they match.
  - In `PlanSummaryCard`:
    - Always render `{workableBeats} remaining` (drop the dual
      "beats"/"remaining" label switch).
    - When `workableBeats === 0`, render a `CheckCircle2` lucide
      icon + a "Done" badge; apply `text-muted-foreground` to the
      objective line for the muted-completion cue (criterion 2).
    - When `workableBeats === 0` AND
      `!isTerminalPlanArtifactState(plan.artifact.state)`, render a
      `Complete` button. Click handler calls a `useMutation` that
      POSTs to the new endpoint, then invalidates the
      `["setlist-plans", repoPath]` query so the row re-renders with
      the artifact in `shipped` and the button gone.
    - Stop click-propagation on the Complete button (the card
      itself is a `<button>` for selection — don't double-fire).

### Server
- `src/lib/orchestration-plan-manager.ts`
  - Add `completePlan(planId, repoPath): Promise<PlanRecord>` that
    looks up the plan, asserts `artifact.state` is non-terminal,
    calls `KnotsBackend.close(planId, { force: true,
    reason: "user_complete_plan" })` (or whatever the existing
    backend signature is — confirm in `src/lib/backends/knots.ts`
    during implementation; do NOT invent a parallel close path),
    then re-loads and returns the updated `PlanRecord`. Throws a
    loud error (matching the project's failure-banner convention)
    if the close fails.
- `src/app/api/plans/[planId]/complete/route.ts` (new file)
  - `POST` handler. Mirrors the shape of the existing plan routes
    (`withServerTiming`, `parseRepoPath`, structured error
    responses). Returns the updated `PlanRecord`. 400 if plan
    already terminal, 404 if not found, 500 otherwise.
- `src/lib/openapi/paths-plans.ts` — register the new route so the
  OpenAPI doc stays in sync (the file already documents
  `/api/plans/{planId}` siblings).

### Tests
- `src/lib/__tests__/setlist-chart.test.ts`
  - Add coverage for `countWorkableBeatIds`: filters terminal
    states, counts unknown beats as workable, dedupes ids.
  - Add `isTerminalPlanArtifactState` cases.
- `src/components/__tests__/setlist-view.test.ts`
  - Update the existing assertions: `4 beats` → `2 remaining`
    (or whatever count the new logic yields with a fixture where
    two of four beats are shipped).
  - Add a test: when `workableBeats === 0` and artifact state is
    non-terminal, the card renders a "Complete" button and a Done
    badge.
  - Add a test: when `workableBeats === 0` and artifact state is
    `shipped`, the Complete button is absent (Done badge stays).
- `src/lib/__tests__/orchestration-plan-manager.test.ts`
  - Add a `completePlan` test using the existing `KnotsBackend`
    mock harness; assert `force: true` was passed.
- `src/lib/__tests__/plans-route.test.ts`
  - Add coverage for `POST /api/plans/[planId]/complete`:
    happy path, already-terminal 400, not-found 404.
- Playwright e2e (manual verification per acceptance criteria):
  - `bun dev` on a non-3000 port, navigate to setlist view.
  - Read collapsed-row badge → click to expand → assert
    badge value matches.
  - Screenshot a fully-shipped plan card; assert Done indicator
    visible.
  - Click `Complete` on a partially-progressed plan with all beats
    shipped; assert artifact.state advances to `shipped` and
    button disappears.
  - Toggle selection back and forth on a once-incorrect setlist;
    assert count is stable (no double-correction — criterion 4).

## Step ordering (single wave, sequential within)

1. Add helpers + new preview field (`setlist-chart.ts`) + their unit
   tests. Run `bun run test` for `setlist-chart.test.ts` only.
2. Wire `PlanSummaryCard` to the new count + Done cue. Update
   component tests. Run `bun run test` for `setlist-view.test.ts`.
3. Add `completePlan` manager function + its tests.
4. Add `POST .../complete` route + its tests + OpenAPI entry.
5. Wire `Complete` button mutation in `PlanSummaryCard`. Confirm
   `useMutation` invalidates `["setlist-plans", repoPath]`.
6. Quality gates: `bun run lint`, `bunx tsc --noEmit`, `bun run test`,
   `bun run build`.
7. Manual Playwright walkthrough on `bun dev` covering all four
   acceptance criteria; capture screenshots.

## Risks / open questions

- **`KnotsBackend.close` signature** — CLAUDE.md cites it as the
  canonical force-close primitive but I haven't read the file. Step 3
  must start by reading `src/lib/backends/knots.ts` to use the real
  API. If `force: true` is not exposed there, fall back to the same
  underlying `kno close --force` shell call the backend wraps; do not
  invent a new mechanism (avoid violating "kno workflows are
  authoritative").
- **Workflow non-terminal artifact states** — the execution_plan_sdlc
  workflow has actions `design`, `review`, `orchestration` and
  terminals `shipped`/`abandoned` plus escapes `blocked`/`deferred`.
  The Complete button intentionally jumps directly to `shipped` from
  any non-terminal state via force-close; this matches the user's
  request ("advances the execution_plan workflow to its positive
  terminal state").
- **beats query loading state** — when `beatsQuery` is still
  loading, the new count helper sees an empty `beatMap` and would
  return 0 for every plan, briefly flashing "0 remaining / Done" on
  unselected rows. Mitigate by short-circuiting in
  `LoadedSetlistView`: if `beatsQuery.isLoading`, render the existing
  loading skeleton/empty state (already happens via
  `getEmptySetlistState`) — confirm this path covers it; if not, gate
  the workable-count map behind `beatsQuery.isSuccess`.
- **Listing cost** — the chosen client-side approach reuses the
  already-loaded `beatMap`, so no new server I/O per plan. If the
  user later wants summaries to be self-sufficient, we can mirror
  the count into `PlanSummary` server-side (`mapPlanSummary` +
  `derivePlanCounts`); that's deliberately out of scope here.
- **Source-contract test guard** at
  `src/components/__tests__/setlist-view.test.ts:188-190` forbids
  `preview.previewBeats.map`, `preview.remainingBeats > 0`,
  `{!selected && (`. The new code must not reintroduce any of those
  patterns — the Done/Complete cues should branch on `workableBeats
  === 0`, not on `selected`.

## Complexity / effort

Medium. ~6 files modified, 1 new route file, 1 new manager function,
~4 test files updated. No schema or migration changes. No new
dependencies. Highest-risk piece is wiring `completePlan` correctly
through `KnotsBackend.close` — must verify the backend signature
before assuming it.
